resharedPost.text:
Folks, I’m looking for your help finding my next adventure. Two options which I can offer right now:

🔹 3-6 months projects in the areas of my key expertise, or
🔹 A long term role with space to grow development skills (preferably Python) which could be done from a different continent in the future—I can elaborate over DM :) Topics closest to my heart: #devtooling / #monitoring / #climate / #education 

In both cases I can offer up to 30hrs/week at this point, starting from September.

How you can help:

🔹 Know someone who could use my help? Please point them to me.
🔹 You could use my help? Let’s chat!
🔹 We've worked together and you had a good experience? Consider writing a LI reference.
🔹 Share this message.
🔹 None of the above, but you still want to say hi? My DMs are always open :)

Thank you all. As you can see, my drawing skills will probably not help me pay rent anytime soon :D

Cheers,
Daria

-----

With over 5 years of hands-on experience in software engineering, my primary focus has been on Site Reliability Engineering (SRE). Throughout my career, I've helped run production systems for diverse organizations, from fast-growing startups to a major public cloud provider. As an operational generalist, I've successfully tackled various aspects, including CI/CD pipelines, automation, infrastructure, monitoring, and alerting, among other critical areas. My archetype would be Organizer.

Not limited to the operational side, I’ve contributed to product development (Node.js), allowing me to empathize with my colleagues on the dev side. My favorite projects to date include introducing Incident Response programs that turn chaos into calm work. I also built an on-call training program that went upstream in an engineering organization of a few thousand people.

In my past life, before switching to engineering, I worked on the business side. From that, I bring years of experience with people in communication, sales, support, and leadership roles. When I need a break from computers I organize things with people (Python conferences, meetups.)

🔹 Experienced in software engineering with a focus on SRE
🔹 Skilled in CI/CD pipelines, automation, infrastructure, monitoring, and alerting
🔹 Hands-on experience implementing SLOs and SLIs
🔹 Empathetic and with experience on the business side
🔹 Experienced in event management (conferences, meetups)
🔹 Enthusiastic about introducing transformative Incident Response programs
🔹 Successful in implementing impactful on-call training initiatives

If you’re looking for someone to help with incident response, on-calls, and production systems in general—let’s chat and explore how I can contribute: hello@dariagrudzien.com

#sre #softwareengineering #incidentresponse #oncall #monitoring #slo #sli #python #iac #cicdpipelines

comments.text:
Good folks with good SRE skills are super hard to find.  Feel free to join Europe Career Support, which is a new community to help folks find work and their next career move.   We have folks in there from Czechia as well and I have a large network of folks based in that country.   You can post into the group and we will highlight it to members and see if can support you

https://www.linkedin.com/groups/12871280/

text:
Hledáte někoho na #SRE nebo #Python? Někoho, kdo umí „you build it, you run it“?

Tady je! Kamarádka a moje bývalá kolegyně z Apiary, Daria Grudzien, hledá nové příležitosti.

A co že je „you build it, you run it“, pokud to neznáte? Zásada vývoje softwaru, která zdůrazňuje odpovědnost vývojového týmu při navrhování, budování a údržbě systémů, které vytváří. V Česku je o tom #YBYR podcast od Ladislav Prskavec a Vilibald W., jiných dvou mých bývalých kolegů z Apiary 😀

Zjevně dobrá líheň odborníků na provoz aplikací, tohleto Apiary! A přesně z téhle líhně Daria je, takže ji fakt chcete mít v týmu.
